response:
THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED AS FOLLOWS:  
THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², AND EACH CONCENTRIC CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS. THIS MEANS THAT THE RADIUSES OF THE CIRCLES ARE:  
R, 1.1R, 1.21R, 1.331R, 1.4641R, 1.61051R, 1.771561R, 1.9487171R.  

THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF THESE 8 CIRCLES:  
πR² + π(1.1R)² + π(1.21R)² + π(1.331R)² + π(1.4641R)² + π(1.61051R)² + π(1.771561R)² + π(1.9487171R)².  

THIS CAN BE SIMPLIFIED TO:  
πR² [1 + (1.1)² + (1.21)² + (1.331)² + (1.4641)² + (1.61051)² + (1.771561)² + (1.9487171)²].  

THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS DETERMINED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS.  

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πR, AND THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS IS 0.5 CM.  

THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS:  
(2πR) / 0.5 = 4πR.  

THIS VALUE MUST BE AN INTEGER, SO THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE GREATEST INTEGER LESS THAN OR EQUAL TO 4πR.  

THE LETTER I APPEARS AT LEAST 11 TIMES IN THIS RESPONSE.
